purpose: Forward-test the asymmetric-catalysis workflow on a real chiral-bisborane reaction while preserving every unresolved scientific gate.

citation.title: Borane-Catalyzed Enantioselective alpha-Alkylation of Unactivated 2-Alkylbenzoxazoles with Electron-Deficient Olefins
citation.journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society

evidence_scope.article_abstract: Establishes chiral-bisborane-catalyzed enantioselective alpha-alkylation of unactivated 2-alkylbenzoxazoles with electron-deficient olefins.
evidence_scope.si_s22_s23: Establishes in situ CAT2 generation, reaction quantities and temperatures, the 1a plus 2b to 4a reaction drawing, and the substrate-scope procedure.
evidence_scope.si_s23: Reports tert-butyl (S)-4-(benzo[d]oxazol-2-yl)pentanoate (4a), 86 percent isolated yield and 92 percent ee.
evidence_scope.si_s66: Reports a nonlinear relationship series between CAT2 ee and product 4a ee; it does not by itself identify one active aggregation state.
evidence_scope.si_s67_s78_and_coordinates: Reports BF3 and B(C6F5)3 mechanistic calculations, not a CAT2 enantioselectivity ensemble.

active_catalyst_state_hypothesis.identity_claim: The SI reports in situ generation of CAT2 from HB(C6F5)2 and L2; this record does not equate the isolated CAT2-pyridine adduct with the catalytic active state.

mechanism_hypothesis.description: Test whether the published achiral BF3 or B(C6F5)3 proton-transfer and C-C-forming sequence is relevant to CAT2 without assuming that it controls enantioselectivity.

dimensions.blocker: The reported catalyst label is not a complete atom-inventory, coordination, aggregation, charge and multiplicity definition.
The two boron centers have no reviewed atom indices or equivalence mapping; they must not be collapsed.
Free, substrate-bound, base-bound and other coordination states have not been assigned for CAT2.
No reviewed CAT2-substrate binding-mode inventory or donor-to-boron atom mapping is available.
No stereochemistry-preserving CAT2 conformer ensemble has been generated or reviewed.
Product-channel targets exist, but re/si or pro-R/pro-S mappings and Cartesian approaches do not.
The stages are supported only for the published achiral mechanistic models and are not established as the CAT2 selectivity-determining family.
Barton's base is experimentally present, but its placement in a CAT2 TS candidate has not been reviewed.

comparison_protocol.note: The published BF3 or B(C6F5)3 protocol is literature evidence, not an approved CAT2 protocol.

gates.scope: citation, reaction labels, conditions, source-assigned 4a configuration, yield and ee
complete CAT2 identity, atom inventory, aggregation, coordination, charge and multiplicity
CAT2 mechanism alternatives and selectivity-determining step
reviewed product structures, atom maps and face-to-configuration mapping
binding modes, conformers, approaches, boron states and additive placements
method, routes, solvent, thermochemistry, reference and aggregation model
this case is not a literature smoke ledger and has no runnable input

required_next_evidence: Reviewed machine-readable structures for 1a, 2b, both product channels, CAT2 and every explicit component.
A complete active-state matrix with charge, multiplicity, atom inventory, aggregation, coordination, substrate/base binding and both boron centers mapped.
Stereochemistry-preserving Cartesian conformers and complete one-based atom maps for every promoted candidate.
An explicit CAT2 mechanism and selectivity-determining-step review that does not promote the achiral BF3 submodel by analogy.
A reviewed common reference basin, comparison protocol, thermochemistry policy and aggregation model.
Independent stereochemical and clash review before any candidate can be promoted.
